name: Portobello Barley Risotto
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound portobello mushrooms, wiped clean with a damp cloth and cut into 1-inch pieces
1/2 cup white wine (Reisling, Sauterne, or dessert white)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 2 tablespoons of oil in a large, heavy pot over medium-high heat. Add the mushroom chunks and cook for 5 to 7 minutes, stirring often. Remove the mushrooms from the pan and set aside.
2. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add remaining oil and the onion. Cook until the onion is soft (about 10 minutes).
3. While cooking the onions, bring the vegetable broth to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan. Adjust the heat to maintain a gentle simmer.
4. Add the barley, rosemary, and allspice to the onions. Raise the heat to medium-high and cook, stirring, for 1 minute, ensuring the barley is evenly coated with oil. Add the wine and cook until nearly evaporated (about 5 minutes).
5. Gradually add the hot broth to the barley mixture, about 1/2 cup at a time, cooking and stirring frequently over medium heat until each addition of broth is absorbed before adding the next.
6. When the barley is tender and nearly all the broth has been absorbed (approximately 45 minutes), stir in the reserved mushrooms and raisins. Cook for a few more minutes until the risotto reaches the desired consistency. Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately, sprinkling with chopped parsley.
Note: When cleaning mushrooms, remove any stem ends. Wipe mushrooms clean with a damp cloth or mushroom brush; do not rinse under running water. Serves 4. Approximately 482 calories, 73 grams carbohydrates, 11 grams protein, 18 grams fat, and 3 milligrams cholesterol.
